title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Only structured party machinery with popularity can ensure success, says Thousand Lights DMK MLA Ezhilan
content: In an interview with The Hindu, the doctor-legislator speaks on his tenure as MLA from Thousand Lights Assembly constituency in Chennai for the first time (to be confirmed by reporter), Vijay’s political entry shaping the 2026 elections, redressal of key grievances and more.

title: Assembly Elections LIVE: DMK president Stalin offers only five seats, CPI (M) yet to decide
content: The Congress and DMK broadly agreed to share 15 seats each in the 30-member Puducherry Assembly, which goes to polls on April 9

title: Four Assam police commandos injured in extremist attack
content: The assailant fired indiscriminately and lobbed several grenades at the commando camp in eastern Assam’s Tinsukia district

title: Even Muslim-majority countries don’t allow Namaz on roads, says Sunil Ambekar of RSS
content: Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh (RSS) national publicity in-charge Sunil Ambekar said that the organisation is working on the ground to promote awareness of shared ancestry and national unity, as highlighted by Sangh chief Mohan Bhagwat

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Sarath Kumar flags lack of roles for him in BJP, voices concern over unfulfilled assurances
content: R. Sarath Kumar said since he joined the BJP, his supporters had been given 19 State-level office-bearer posts, while postings for 251 persons had been sought and only one-fourth of them had been granted.

title: DMK and its smaller allies: Ideologically aligned, politically squeezed
content: DMK-led Secular Progressive Alliance under Chief Minister M.K. Stalin has held together for nearly nine years, with its durability stemming in large part from a shared anti-Hindutva ideological alignment among its constituents. Yet, this cohesion has not come without a cost.

title: Kharat case: SIT probe intensifies; temple priest, watchman among those questioned
content: A day earlier, a staffer of Kharat's office was questioned for more than seven hours by the SIT led by Indian Police Service officer Tejaswini Satpute, they added

title: Hospital merger to boost MBBS, PG seats, and specialised training: Rekha Gupta
content: Delhi CM says integration of GTB Hospital, DSCI and RGSSH on AIIMS model will expand medical education, research and healthcare capacity

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026: Kunnathur constituency all in for a factional duel
content: Kunnathur’s political identity has been inseparable from the RSP for decades.  However, a split occurred when the official RSP faction moved from LDF to UDF in 2016,  with Kovoor Kunjumon forming RSP (Leninist) to remain with LDF. Since then, the constituency has been a primary battleground for two RSP factions

title: House sparrow population plummets in Thiruvananthapuram, finds survey
content: Conservationists point to rapid urbanisation, loss of nesting spaces, scarcity of food and competition from pigeons as primary causes for population decline. Environmentalists propose incorporating bird-friendly features in upcoming infrastructure projects to revive bird population in Thiruvananthapuram

title: Good beginning, talks to proceed: Manipur CM on meeting with Kuki Zo Council delegation
content: Addressing the media on March 22, the Chief Minister said, "In Manipur's situation, the most saddening part is that internally displaced persons (IDPs) are unable to return to their native homes...”

title: K.C. Tyagi joins Rashtriya Lok Dal days after quitting JD(U)
content: A founding member of the JD(U), he did not participate in the party’s membership renewal drive, signalling his exit from the party on March 17

title: Indo-Tibet trade to resume via Shipki La from June after Covid pause: Jagat Singh Negi
content: Mr. Negi also urged the Union government to develop the Shipki La pass road to facilitate the Kailash-Mansarovar Yatra

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Velmurugan’s Tamilaga Vazhvurimai Katchi exits DMK alliance; rules out joining NDA
content: Mr. Velmurugan, accusing DMK president and Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M.K. Stalin of displaying “big brotherly attitude,” charged the DMK has succumbed to the pressures of bureaucrats “influenced and guided by RSS ideology” in not conducting caste-wise census.

title: Security personnel unearth country-made gun making unit set by Maoists in Odisha's Kandhamal
content: Security personnel from SOG, BSF, CRPF and other forces were pressed into the anti-Maoist operation in an organised manner in an effort to achieve the target to eliminate Maoists by the end of this month, they said

title: Environmental activist expresses concern at degradation of Kolleru Lake
content: The approximate lake area was 2,22,600 acres in 1975 and today approximately  approximately 1,28,000 acres is under aquaculture/ encroachment, National convenor of Jal Biradari Bolisetty Satyanarayana said

title: Mangaluru-Moodbidri NH 169 portion near Yedapadavu opens for traffic after month-long closure
content: The National Highways Authority of India has completed construction of one of the two-carriageways of Yedapadavu bypass facilitating traffic movement

title: Kannada Development Authority Chairman releases book on ‘Bhootaradhane’
content: Author Poovappa Kaniyoor has revised and refined his research done 22 years ago and published it after it has matured.

title: What did the Supreme Court say about paid maternity leave?
content: Why did the Supreme Court strike down Section 60(4) of the Social Security Code, 2020? What did it say about maternity leave for adoptive mothers? What did the Court recommend that the Union government do regarding paternity leave?

title: Environmentalists seek conservation of Slender Loris in Madurai’s Melur region
content: In the regions of Kesampatti, Pattur, Sekkipatti, Kambur, Pulipatti and Melavalavu within the Melur taluk, Grey Slender Loris, a species of primate in the family Loridae, is found in significant numbers.

title: 2026 Assembly elections schedule: Dates for Assam, West Bengal, Kerala, Tamil Nadu and Puducherry
content: CEC Gyanesh Kumar on March 15, 2026, announced that the 2026 State Assembly elections will take place in 824 constituencies, with approximately 17.4 crore eligible voters participating in the democratic process

title: Death toll rises to 16 in Andhra milk adulteration case
content: Laboratory findings show that the 16 victims died of multi-organ failure triggered by acute renal failure after consuming milk contaminated with the toxic substance; police took the suspected milk vendor into custody, while forensic teams examined the premises and collected evidence.

title: Kerala State Electricity Board seeks regulator’s nod for medium-term power purchase deal with Damodar Valley Corporation
content: The power utility plans to procure 25 MW from the  Damodar Valley Corporation’s Koderma Thermal Power Station and 55 MW from Mejia Thermal Power Station

title: West Asia conflict: Crude oil vessel from Russia, LPG ship from U.S. begin unloading cargo in Mangaluru
content: The Shipping Ministry informed that New Mangalore Port has waived cargo-related charges for crude oil and LPG from March 14 to 31.

title: Cabinet colleagues laud PM Modi’s record breaking 8,931 days in public office
content: His Cabinet colleagues congratulated Prime Minister Modi on this record, with Defence Minister Rajnath Singh lauding his “pure devotion to the nation and its people” both as Chief Minister of Gujarat and as PM

title: West Asia conflict: PM Modi calls ministerial meeting to review energy situation
content: The focus of the meeting is to ensure uninterrupted supply, stable logistics and efficient distribution across the country, and the government is taking proactive steps to this end, they said

content: Mr. Shanmugam said, though both parties had been holding talks, a meeting with the Chief Minister had taken place only now. “As he has requested us, we have to respect his words and hold further talks,” he said.

title: Global crisis deepens due to war; CPI slams Modi’s silence
content: India, as a major democracy, should play a proactive role in promoting peace, says CPI leader K. Narayana

title: Telangana Govt raises TDR use, allows more floors and setback relaxations in new building rules
content: The new norms were issued on March 22, 2026 by the Municipal Administration and Urban Development (MA&UD) Department.

title: 27-year-old man killed in early morning attack in east Bengaluru
content: Investigation revealed that the accused and deceased were from the same area and had financial disputes. Police suspect that a financial feud may be the motive behind the murder; however, this is yet to be confirmed.

title: Teen killed, four others critically injured as speeding car rams auto in Vikarabad
content: The accident was reported around 7 p.m. near Rakamcherla village in Pudur mandal when the victims were travelling from Hyderabad towards Parigi to attend a family wedding in an autorickshaw

title: Lamborghini stunt in Bengaluru: Police serve notice to Muthappa Rai’s son Ricky Rai
content: Police said the high-end car was traced to Ricky Rai’s residence in Sadashivanagar and has been seized. However, there is no clarity on who owns the vehicle or whether it was being driven by Mr. Rai.

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election 2026: Thousand Lights, a developed neighbourhood that struggles with traffic woes
content: By virtue of its key location, the Thousand Lights Assembly segment has long been at the epicentre of vibrant political activity. The constituency is also home to several iconic places.

title: Tomato farmers hope for a ‘bright’ summer in Madanapalle
content: Horticulture officials said the season remained largely free from extreme weather conditions and they expect stable weather till May to support yields

title: Himanta, Assam BJP chief Dilip Saikia reach out to leaders threatening to contest as Independents
content: The recent induction of Congress leaders Pradyut Bordoloi and Bhupen Borah into the BJP and their subsequent nomination as party candidates has raised the hackles of many ticket aspirants in the party

title: Supreme Court directs States, UTs to furnish updated details on prisons, including overcrowding
content: It said the data shall include jail-wise capacity of each prison, total number of prisoners, percentage of overcrowding in each jail, steps proposed to address overcrowding, and details of women’s jails

title: Mizoram civic body election: Campaign gains momentum as parties gear up for April 21 polls
content: The last date for filing nomination papers is March 25, while the nominees can withdraw their candidatures latest by March 30, officials said

title: Karnataka bypolls: Congress keeps it within family; nominates Umesh Meti and Samarth Mallikarjun
content: Umesh Meti will fight to retain his father H.Y. Meti’s seat and Samarth Mallikarjun will enter the fray to protect his grandfather Shamanur Shivashankarappa’s legacy.

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election: VCK under compulsion to consider local candidates, says Thirumavalavan
content: He also recalls the opinion of the DMK that the party lost in the Arakkonam and Vanur constituencies because its candidates were outsiders

title: Rice, wheat procurement remains ‘consistently’ low; House panel raises concern
content: Kanimozhi-led panel asks Union Food Ministry to strengthen its procurement planning and coordination with States to minimise the gap between estimated and actual procurement

title: T.N. Assembly election: parties must avoid excessive freebies, focus on development, says AIADMK leader Semmalai
content: Contending that these handouts being announced as part of electoral promises by major parties will not facilitate T.N.’s development, he criticises the DMK government for not increasing the capital expenditure substantially over the last 5 years and ‘frittering away’ funds for distributing freebies

title: Jharkhand Police seek army help to defuse suspected WWII-era bomb
content: East Singhbhum SP (Rural) Rishav Garg said the bomb detection and disposal squad (BDDS) from Ranchi inspected the site in Baharagora on Thursday and found the device to be "active" and heavy.

title: 275 former Judges, officials slam U.S. international religious freedom report
content: A total of 275 signatories include 25 retired judges, 119 retired bureaucrats, including 10 ambassadors, and 131 armed forces officers

title: Meghalaya leader of Opposition Mukul Sangma demands independent inquiry into Garo Hills violence
content: In a petition to Governor C.H. Vijayashankar, Mr. Sangma flagged a "serious law and order situation" in the Garo Hills region, which he said stemmed from a series of developments linked to the elections to the tribal Garo Hills Autonomous District Council

title: Congress fields T. Chalukumba Ao for Koridang bypoll in Nagaland
content: State Congress president S. Supongmeren Jamir formally handed over the symbol to Mr. Ao at the party's State headquarters in the presence of working presidents Khriedi Theunuo and C. Apok Jamir

title: Two from Karnataka killed in road accident near Chittoor; four others injured
content: The Puthalapattu circle police said the car, bearing a Karnataka registration number, hit a 10-wheeler lorry as the driver reportedly felt drowsy

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election 2026: a game of four
content: The Tamil Nadu Assembly election will see a four-cornered contest this April. As the DMK and its allies and the AIADMK and its partners remain the principal participants of the big fight, it is also clear that the newly-formed Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam and the steady presence of the Naam Tamizhar Katchi in electoral politics will have roles to play. This report analyses the political situation ahead of this election that entices voters with the promise that it will be like nothing the State has never seen so far.

title: DU’s job fair guidelines stir row over ‘routine exclusion’ of Open Learning students
content: The protesters argue that students of the School of Open Learning are usually from marginalised communities and deserve the same opportunities. There are currently around 4 lakh SOL students, according to officials.

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election: CPI(M) State Committee stands firm on six seats
content: The CPI(M)’s contention is that it had been promised an increase in the seats for the 2021 Tamil Nadu Assembly election by the DMK leadership. On the other hand, the DMK argues that it has included more political parties in the alliance and is likely to lose 15 constituencies from its share

title: Mild earthquake of 4.1 magnitude reported in Sikkim; no casualties
content: The epicenter of the earthquake was in Mangan district at 14 km below the surface at 8.41 p.m. on Saturday (March 21, 2026)

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election 2026: Stalemate in DMK-CPI(M) seat-sharing continues
content: The delay in finalising seat-sharing has stalled the next phase of identifying constituencies and announcing candidates

content: Here's how every second-round game was won Saturday, plus how each winner can go beyond the Sweet 16.

title: Arozarena apologizes to Raleigh over WBC spat
content: Randy Arozarena says he has apologized to Cal Raleigh for the expletive-laced comments directed at his Mariners teammate earlier this month at the WBC.

title: Sources: Providence to hire Hodgson as new HC
content: Providence is set to hire South Florida's Bryan Hodgson as the program's new head coach, sources confirmed to ESPN.

title: Huskers survive after Vanderbilt's heave rims out
content: Nebraska outlasted Vanderbilt 74-72 in a scintillating second-round NCAA tournament game Saturday night when Tyler Tanner's heave from the half-court line rimmed out at the buzzer.

title: Sources: Lakers appeal tech with Luka facing ban
content: Luka Doncic faces a one-game suspension after a verbal exchange with Orlando's Goga Bitadze earned him his 16th technical foul of the season Saturday night. Sources told ESPN the Lakers have already filed an appeal.

title: 'Four more to go': KD passes MJ on scoring list
content: Kevin Durant passed Michael Jordan for fifth on the NBA's career scoring list after finishing with 27 points in a win over the Heat.

title: Miller: Texas not Cinderella despite Sweet 16 run
content: Texas went from the NCAA tournament's First Four to the Sweet 16, but coach Sean Miller said his team isn't a Cinderella after upsetting No. 3 seed Gonzaga on Saturday.

title: Sources: UNC, Davis discussing future after loss
content: UNC officials are in discussions with coach Hubert Davis about the coach's future after another first-round loss in the NCAA tournament.

title: Brady shines, Team USA dominates flag football
content: Tom Brady started out like the GOAT of flag football before Team USA dominated the Fanatics Flag Football Classic on Saturday.

title: 💰 Men's tournament best bets: Arizona, Kentucky
content: As you prepare for tipoff, here are our best bets for the second round, plus a pick for the new Eliminator game.

content: Sunday's Carabao Cup final could help tell us whether Arsenal can win the Premier League or if this might be the end for Pep Guardiola at Man City.

content: The top four teams are in action Sunday. Plus, updated playoff projections, draft lottery standings after a big Saturday.

content: What did you miss at major league camps this spring while watching the World Baseball Classic? Here's what stood out to us from Florida and Arizona.

content: Who does Tristan H. Cockcroft think will underachieve in 2026? We have the names to steer clear of when drafting your rosters.

content: Luka Doncic is on another planet, and Ayo Dosunmu and Daniss Jenkins are on the rise, but should fantasy basketball managers keep Giannis Antetokounmpo and Mikal Bridges rostered?

title: Transfer rumors, news: Liverpool eye Newcastle's Gordon to replace Gakpo
content: Liverpool are considering Newcastle United's Anthony Gordon as a replacement for Cody Gakpo. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Farmers, artisans should find innovative ways to market their produce, says ZP CEO
content: e-Paper The View From India Looking at World Affairs from the Indian perspective . first day first show news and reviews from the world of cinema and streaming . the weekly newsletter from science writers takes the jargon out of science and puts the fun in!

title: 'Europe within Iranian missiles range': IDF warns after Diego Garcia strike; UK says 'no evidence'
content: Iranian forces launched long-range missiles for the first time, striking near Israel's nuclear site and injuring dozens. The missiles, capable of reaching European capitals, were fired at the US-UK base Diego Garcia. These actions escalate the conflict, with Iran stating they targeted military installations in response to US-Israeli attacks.

title: 'Why don’t you just die': India star reveals shocking death threats and abuse
content: Spinner Varun Chakravarthy revealed the severe online abuse he endured after testing positive for COVID-19 during the 2021 IPL, which led to the tournament's suspension. Fans directed hateful messages, including death wishes, towards him. Despite this traumatic experience, Chakravarthy has since become a key player for KKR and performed well in the recent T20 World Cup.

title: 'Kicked and punched': Sikh student tortured, turban forcibly removed in Pakistan
content: A Sikh girl was allegedly assaulted at a Christian school in Faisalabad, Pakistan, after refusing to participate in prayers. Her turban was reportedly removed, and she was beaten. The incident has sparked outrage, with a community group demanding action. The girl's mother also recounted past abuse, raising concerns about minority safety.

title: ‘We will help fulfil Chaudhary Charan Singh’s dream’: KC Tyagi after joining RLD
content: Senior JD(U) leader KC Tyagi has joined the Rashtriya Lok Dal, aiming to uphold Chaudhary Charan Singh's legacy. Tyagi, whose political roots are in western Uttar Pradesh, stated his move signifies continuity, not change, emphasizing the ideological closeness between JD(U) and RLD. He clarified his departure wasn't driven by personal friction or Rajya Sabha ambitions.

title: Tesla finance VP Sendil Palani exits, recalls Tesla Deathwatch' days
content: After seventeen years, Tesla's senior finance member Sendil Palani has departed, having joined when the company was on the brink of collapse in late 2008. Palani lauded his finance team for making complex tasks appear effortless and credited cross-functional late-night sessions for invaluable real-world learning. He thanked CEO Elon Musk for championing first-principles thinking.

title: PSL 2026 behind closed doors; Mohsin Naqvi warns of action against players joining IPL
content: Pakistan Super League 2026 will commence on March 26 behind closed doors due to regional tensions, with matches limited to Karachi and Lahore. The Pakistan Cricket Board cited government austerity measures, not security fears, for the decision. The tournament's opening ceremony has been canceled, and refunds will be issued to ticket holders.

title: Beyond a diagnosis: Rethinking Down Syndrome in a more inclusive world
content: World Down Syndrome Day on March 21 highlights Trisomy 21, a genetic condition affecting millions. While biology is understood, outcomes depend on care, early intervention, and societal perception. Early diagnosis and therapies are crucial for individuals to achieve milestones and participate fully in life.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO Day 4 eyes Rs 400 cr India, Rs 500 cr+ worldwide collection
content: The box office tsunami unleashed by Aditya Dhar’s espionage thriller, 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge,' has officially crossed the monumental Rs. 500 crore mark in global gross collections on its first Sunday. As of Day 4 live tracking on March 22, 2026, the Ranveer Singh-starrer continues its historic rampage, netting above Rs. 360 crore in India. Despite its 'A' certificate and massive 229-minute runtime, the film has shattered records previously held by 'RRR' and 'KGF: Chapter 2', fueled by a visceral performance from Singh as the deep-cover operative Hamza.

title: Virat Kohli reacts to viral 'chartered London flights' rumour ahead of IPL 2026
content: Virat Kohli has debunked rumors about requesting chartered flights for travel between India and the UK during IPL 2026. The RCB icon dismissed the speculation with laughing emojis on Instagram. The IPL 2026 season begins March 28, with RCB defending their title.

title: Elon Musk's SpaceX sends email to FCC, rejects Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin application
content: SpaceX has formally responded to Amazon's FCC petition against its satellite data center proposal. SpaceX argues that if Amazon's criticisms are applied, they must also be applied to Blue Origin's similar satellite data center application. SpaceX is pushing for equitable treatment and consistent review of both companies' plans.

title: ​Blue saree brigade: Women at the heart of India’s water systems
content: Women make up less than 17 percent of the paid water workforce globally — but in India's villages, they are already running the systems. This is the story of how grassroots networks and government schemes are turning water's biggest burden-bearers into its most effective managers.

title: Why Mumbai court acquitted bizman in 2013 suicide case
content: A Mumbai court acquitted a businessman accused of abetting his HIV-positive ex-wife's suicide, ruling mere harassment insufficient. The 12-year case hinged on allegations of abuse and threats. The judge emphasized the need for clear intent and direct instigation, finding prosecution evidence lacking on both counts, leading to reasonable doubt and acquittal.

title: Market recap: Mcap of five of top-10 most valued firms erodes by Rs 1 lakh cr
content: Dalal Street experienced a significant market capitalisation erosion of over Rs 1 lakh crore for five top firms last week, with HDFC Bank bearing the brunt. Despite initial gains, selling pressure in later sessions led to a muted market close, reflecting participant caution.

title: AA22xA6: Anushka Sharma's Telugu debut with Allu Arjun, Deepika - Report
content: Allu Arjun's upcoming film, tentatively titled 'AA22xA6' with director Atlee, is reportedly assembling a star-studded cast. Following reports of Deepika Padukone, fresh buzz suggests Anushka Sharma might join the ambitious sci-fi project, marking her potential Telugu debut. The film is also rumored to feature Mrunal Thakur, Janhvi Kapoor, and Rashmika Mandanna.

title: Teacher thrashes student with cane at Ujjain school; probe ordered after video goes viral
content: A disturbing video emerged showing a teacher caning a student at a Ujjain residential school. The clip, now viral, depicts the educator repeatedly striking the child in an office. Police have launched a preliminary investigation into the incident, believed to have occurred around March 15th. Authorities are gathering details on those involved.

title: KKR's playing XI for IPL 2026: Harshit, Pathirana out; Kartik Tyagi to debut
content: Kolkata Knight Riders face pace bowling woes for IPL 2026 with key players injured. Despite a massive auction spend, including record signing Cameron Green, the team's strategy is reshaped.  The predicted XI features new faces and retained stars, with Umran Malik poised for an impact role in a bid for redemption.

title: Upcoming week at Dalal Street: Middle East conflict, crude oil trends to guide markets
content: Investor sentiment remains cautious as the Middle East conflict enters its fourth week, impacting global stock markets and crude oil prices.  For the upcoming holiday-shortened week, market direction will be guided by geopolitical developments, foreign investor activity, and rupee fluctuations.  Incoming data and global uncertainties are expected to drive market sensitivity.

title: 8,931 days milestone! PM Modi become longest-serving head of government
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has become India's longest-serving head of government, completing 8,931 days in office. This milestone surpasses the previous record held by Pawan Kumar Chamling. Modi's extensive public service includes over 13 years as Gujarat's Chief Minister and his tenure as Prime Minister since 2014.

title: Sharjeel Imam returns home after 6 years on interim bail for brother’s wedding
content: Sharjeel Imam, an accused in the "larger conspiracy" case related to the Delhi riots of 2020, reached his ancestral village Kako after six years to attend his younger brother Muzzammil Imam's wedding and spend time with his ailing mother. The Imam arrived here following a 10-day interim bail by a Delhi Court.

title: March FPI outflow: Foreign investors pull out Rs 88,180 cr amid Middle East tensions
content: Foreign investors have withdrawn Rs 88,180 crore from Indian equities in March, driven by rising global tensions, a weakening rupee, and high oil prices. This sharp outflow follows a strong February and highlights investor caution due to geopolitical risks and concerns over corporate earnings. Analysts anticipate continued market volatility until global uncertainties ease.

title: Why Kerala's 'semi-final' verdict has put Left on red alert
content: Local body poll results in Kerala signal a potential shift, with the Congress-led UDF gaining momentum against the ruling LDF. The UDF secured key urban strongholds, raising concerns about anti-incumbency for Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan's government. The BJP also shows strategic expansion, hinting at a more competitive assembly election.

title: $800 million blow! US military infrastructure in Middle East heavily damaged from Iran strikes
content: A devastating Middle East war, now in its third week, sees no de-escalation from the US, Israel, or Iran. Iranian strikes have inflicted an estimated $800 million in damage to US bases, targeting critical radar and communication systems.  Aircraft losses mount for both sides, with significant financial strain on the US.

title: US House resolution seeks ‘genocide’ tag for Pakistan’s 1971 atrocities on Bengali Hindus
content: US Congressman Greg Landsman has introduced a resolution in the House of Representatives seeking to recognise the atrocities committed by the Pakistani army and its allies against Bengali Hindus in 1971 as war crimes and genocide. The resolution cites the 'Blood Telegram' and details the systematic targeting of Hindus for extermination, urging condemnation of these actions

title: Rs 27 crore tag! Rishabh Pant 'under most pressure' in IPL 2026
content: Rishabh Pant faces immense pressure heading into IPL 2026 after a disappointing 2025 season with Lucknow Super Giants. Bought for a record Rs 27 crore, Pant's batting form was underwhelming, and LSG failed to reach the playoffs. Former captain Faf du Plessis believes Pant's captaincy and batting performance are under the spotlight.

title: Tamil Nadu polls: TVK chief T Velmurugan quits DMK alliance over seat-sharing row
content: Tamizhaga Vazhvurimai Katchi (TVK) founder T Velmurugan has exited the DMK-led alliance over seat-sharing disputes, stating his party won't join the BJP either due to religious division. Velmurugan expressed frustration with the DMK's "big brother attitude" and alleged Chief Minister Stalin is influenced by RSS-aligned officials on caste surveys. TVK will decide its next move, potentially contesting alone.

title: Dialling down: Middle East tensions raise alarm for India's smartphone shipments
content: India's smartphone market faces a grim outlook as the Middle East crisis impacts demand and supply. Analysts have slashed shipment forecasts for 2026, citing rising essential goods prices and component shortages. The conflict's ripple effects, including disrupted trade routes and potential helium supply issues, are expected to worsen the situation, particularly in the latter half of the year.

title: IPL 2026: Five pacers in race as KKR look for Harshit Rana replacement
content: Kolkata Knight Riders are scrambling for replacements ahead of IPL 2026 after Harshit Rana's injury. Mid-preseason trials at Eden Gardens are underway, with Navdeep Saini and Akash Madhwal among those being assessed. The team also faces concerns with Mustafizur Rahman's departure and Matheesha Pathirana's delayed return, intensifying their bowling worries.

title: A will, blamed for husband's suicide and more: Chilling details emerge from Kerala house of horror
content: A chilling discovery in Kochi: five family members, including three young children, were found dead in their rented home. Police suspect the children were poisoned before the women died by suicide, with a suicide note pointing to severe family tensions following the husband's death. The family had recently moved to Kochi for a child's treatment.

title: Sky‑high losses: Iran war drives airlines to biggest crash since Covid – $50bn gone
content: Global airlines face their worst financial crisis since COVID-19 due to the Iran conflict, wiping over $50 billion off major carriers' market value. Soaring jet fuel prices, now double, threaten ticket price hikes and potential shortages. Executives warn of dampened demand from sustained high fares, while Middle Eastern airlines are hit particularly hard by airspace closures and tourism collapse.

title: Under the sea: How Iran's invisible fleet of 'midget submarines' threatens Strait of Hormuz
content: As Middle East tensions rise, Iran's hidden underwater threats, particularly its Ghadir-class midget submarines and extensive naval mine stockpiles, pose a significant challenge to global shipping. These stealthy assets, capable of operating in shallow waters and laying mines, can disrupt vital oil transit routes like the Strait of Hormuz, impacting global trade far more effectively than visible attacks.

title: Puducherry polls: BJP names another candidate for Karaikal South - check full list
content: The BJP has finalized its Puducherry assembly election candidates, naming M Arulmurugan for Karaikal South. This completes their list for the April 9 polls, where they contest 10 seats alongside allies AINRC, AIADMK, and LJK. Preparations are in full swing for the 30-seat election, with EVMs and VVPATs readied for over 9.44 lakh voters.

title: MBBS graduate ends life after family objects to adopting cat in Hyderabad
content: A 23-year-old MBBS graduate in Hyderabad died by suicide after a dispute with her family over keeping a pet cat. The family reportedly opposed the cat due to allergy concerns. Upset by the disagreement, she took her own life while her family was out.

title: Kerala: Woman dresses kids in suits, makes them wear goggles before killing them; 5 found dead
content: A grim discovery in Kochi's Vaduthala saw five family members, including three children, found dead in their rented home. Preliminary findings suggest poisoning for the children, while the women were found hanging. A suicide note points to immense personal distress, including the recent death of the husband. Investigations are ongoing.

title: Is Justin Timberlake and Jessica Biel's marriage at risk amid his DUI controversy?
content: Newly released bodycam footage from Justin Timberlake's 2024 DUI arrest shows the singer struggling through sobriety tests. Despite a light plea deal, the viral clip reignites scrutiny on his career and his 13-year marriage to Jessica Biel, who has publicly defended their relationship amidst persistent split rumors.

title: Ranking the remaining 32 teams in women's March Madness
content: only three lower-seeded teams won games in the first two days of the tournament . the Cavaliers are just the second team since the field expanded to 68 teams in 2022 to go from the first four to the second round . south Carolina 's 69-point trouncing was the most statistically impressive victory among the top seeds .

title: Men's March Madness live updates: Previews, action from all of Sunday's games
content: the first half of the Sweet 16 is set, with 16 teams playing for one of the final eight tickets to the second weekend of the 2026 men's NCAA tournament . if Braden Smith controls the pace of the matchup, its frontcourt gets the upper hand over the frontcourt . the Boilermakers can beat anyone -- they're a tough team to stall with their size inside and Smith's ability to dictate the flow of any game.

title: Women's March Madness Day 2 takeaways: How UConn, USC, Virginia won
